patch.value: ADR: pm extensions & SDK packages must be authored fully in TypeScript

Policy ADR: every pm extension and SDK package MUST be authored fully in TypeScript (TS source compiled to a JS entry the loader imports), never plain JavaScript, to guarantee full type safety and contract/spec enforcement at author time. Aligns the pm extension/package init scaffolder with the first-party packages (index.ts compiled to index.js) and reframes the SDK authoring docs/docstrings TypeScript-first.

## Context

The SDK authoring surface (`define*` builders, `composeExtension`, the modular-authoring primitives) and the `pm extension/package init` scaffolder were historically framed around **plain JavaScript** packages — the scaffolder emits `index.js` with a single JSDoc `@param {import("...").ExtensionApi}` annotation, and the SDK docs/docstrings justify the `define*` helpers with "plain-JavaScript packages cannot write TS annotations."

This is inconsistent with reality: every first-party package (`packages/pm-*`) is authored in **TypeScript** — `index.ts` source importing SDK types from `src/sdk/index.js`, compiled to `index.js` (the manifest `entry`) via `tsconfig.packages.json`. A plain-JS authoring path forfeits the very thing the SDK exists to provide: end-to-end type safety and compile-time enforcement of the registration contracts/specifications.

## Decision

Every pm extension and SDK package MUST be authored **fully in TypeScript**, never plain JavaScript:

1. Source is `index.ts` (and any siblings) written in TypeScript, importing types from `@unbrained/pm-cli/sdk`.
2. The TypeScript is compiled to a JavaScript entry (`./index.js`) that the manifest `entry` points to and the loader imports — the loader continues to import compiled JS; TypeScript is the authoring layer, not a runtime loader feature.
3. `pm extension init` / `pm package init` scaffold TypeScript source + a `tsconfig.json` + a `build` script, mirroring the first-party packages.
4. All SDK docs (`docs/SDK.md`, `docs/EXTENSIONS.md`), docstrings, and examples are reframed TypeScript-first. The `define*` / `defineExtensionBlueprint` identity helpers are justified by their TypeScript value — preserving narrow literal types while checking against the interface, contextual handler-parameter inference, colocated/exported/unit-testable definitions (the Vite `defineConfig` pattern) — not by "JS can't annotate."

## Rationale

- Full type safety is the SDK's core value proposition; TypeScript enforces the registration contracts (`CommandDefinition`, `ExtensionManifest`, capability sets) at author time instead of deferring failures to `api.register*`/load time.
- The first-party packages already prove the `index.ts` → compiled `index.js` pattern; standardizing the scaffolder on it removes the JS/TS split-brain and gives external authors a path identical to the maintainers'.
- Identity helpers (`define*`) remain valuable in TypeScript: they are the idiomatic way to get literal-type preservation + inference without losing the interface check, and to make definitions exportable/unit-testable apart from `activate`.

## Alternatives considered

- **Keep the JS-first scaffold, document TS as optional.** Rejected: undermines the type-safety guarantee and perpetuates the split-brain with first-party packages.
- **Load TypeScript directly at runtime (tsx/transpile-on-load).** Rejected: adds a runtime transpile dependency and startup cost; compiled JS entry is simpler, faster, and matches the existing loader.

## Consequences

- `pm extension/package init` scaffolds `index.ts` + `tsconfig.json` + `build` script; the manifest `entry` stays `./index.js` (compiled output); the README documents `npm install && npm run build` before load.
- Scaffold tests, smoke (`smoke:external-packages`), and dogfood (`dogfood:package-first`) flows must build the TypeScript scaffold before loading.
- SDK docs and docstrings are reframed TypeScript-first; the legacy "plain-JS authors" framing is removed as files are touched.
- Tracked by the TypeScript-first scaffolding feature and the modular declarative-authoring feature (pm-2p38).

author: claude-code-agent
message: Link ADR to the features that implement it

- Tracked by the TypeScript-first scaffolding feature and the modular declarative-authoring feature (pm-2p38).

## Fully implemented (2026-06-24)

The last artifacts still authored in plain JavaScript — the committed docs/examples reference extensions (starter-extension, policy-restricted-extension) — are now TypeScript-first (pm-ax7z): `index.ts` source authored against `@unbrained/pm-cli/sdk`, compiled to the committed `./index.js` entry the loader imports, type-checked centrally via the new `tsconfig.examples.json` project, and coverage-tracked through the `.ts` source exactly like the first-party `packages/pm-*`.

The decision is now realized end-to-end:
- Scaffolder (`pm extension/package init`) emits TypeScript source + tsconfig + build (pm-09rh).
- First-party packages (`packages/pm-*`) are `index.ts` → compiled `index.js`.
- docs/examples reference extensions are TypeScript-first (pm-ax7z).
- SDK authoring docs/docstrings are reframed TypeScript-first; `define*`/`defineExtension(Blueprint)` identity helpers are justified by their type-level value, not by "JS can't annotate."

Marking this ADR accepted/closed; the policy remains in force via the scaffolder, the `tsc -p tsconfig.{packages,examples}.json` type-check gates, and the SDK docs.

Accepted and fully implemented: scaffolder, first-party packages, and now docs/examples reference extensions (pm-ax7z) are all TypeScript-first; SDK docs/docstrings reframed TypeScript-first. Policy enforced via the scaffolder and the tsc -p tsconfig.{packages,examples}.json type-check gates.

Accepted and fully implemented: scaffolder, first-party packages, and now docs/examples reference extensions (pm-ax7z) are all TypeScript-first; SDK docs/docstrings reframed TypeScript-first. Policy enforced via the scaffolder and the tsc -p tsconfig.{packages,examples}.json type-check gates.
Every pm extension and SDK package authored fully in TypeScript across scaffolder, first-party packages, and docs/examples; SDK docs reframed TypeScript-first.

Decision realized end-to-end; the final plain-JS artifacts (docs/examples reference extensions) converted in pm-ax7z; enforced by the scaffolder and the tsconfig.{packages,examples}.json type-check gates.

## Superseded in part (2026-06-24) by [[pm-m1uz]]

This ADR's "authored fully in TypeScript" policy **remains in force**. However, its loading-model consequence — "the manifest `entry` stays `./index.js` (compiled output)" and the rejection of "Load TypeScript directly at runtime" — is **superseded by [[pm-m1uz]]** (implemented by [[pm-2p7a]]).

Node native type stripping (Node >=22.18, dependency-free) makes the rejected "load `.ts` directly" alternative viable without a runtime transpile dependency. Extensions/packages now ship the `.ts` source as the manifest `entry` and the loader imports it directly — there is no compiled `./index.js`. The "authored in TypeScript" decision here is unchanged; only "compiled to a JS entry" is replaced by "the `.ts` IS the entry".
